Quickly becoming one of my favorite games. If you like good stories in general, this game is worth every cent.

10/10

This game is organized military chaos at its absolute peak. This masterpiece somehow took everything from COD4 and cranked it to absurd levels without completely losing that grounded military vibe.

The mission run is just straight-up illegal. No Russian? Still one of the most infamous missions in gaming history. And even the mission before it perfectly builds up that feeling that something really bad is about to happen. You just know the game is cooking something.

Then suddenly the game throws you into complete urban warfare insanity: defending Burger Town with a drone and Stinger missiles while everything around you is exploding? Peak MW2 energy. Absolute panic simulator.

And the whole Washington storyline? BRO.

Defending the Washington Monument while the city turns into a battlefield felt insane. The scenario of taking back Washington was pure military movie material. Helicopters everywhere, soldiers moving through destroyed streets, radio chatter nonstop… it felt like controlled chaos in the best way possible.

Then the EMP explosion hits…

BROOOOO.

Watching helicopters drop from the sky and seeing modern warfare instantly turn into complete confusion was one of those gaming moments that permanently lives in your head rent free.

The infiltration sections also go unbelievably hard. Snow missions, stealth phases, sneaking behind enemy lines — classic Task Force 141 stuff. And getting to use guided rocket launchers made you feel like you were carrying military technology from the year 3000.

The military realism mixed with Hollywood-level spectacle somehow works perfectly. It constantly throws ridiculous scenarios at you but still sells them with enough seriousness that you completely buy into it.

AND THEN…

THAT ENDING.

That betrayal by General Shepherd?

Nah.

Absolutely unforgettable.

I remember sitting there staring at my screen thinking:

“NO WAY THIS MF ACTUALLY DID THAT.”

Gaming betrayal Hall of Fame material.

Overall:
infiltration missions, urban warfare, military realism, unforgettable set pieces, emotional damage, and one of the greatest campaign rides ever made.

“Good… that’s one less loose end.”

Still hurts.

Ah, yes. Modern Warfare 2. The Michael Bay of Call of Duty Campaigns. What could possibly be said about it that hasn't already been discussed?

Perhaps it's best to start with the single-player portion of the game. The campaign, for me personally, holds incredibly well and still has my testosterone levels going up every single time Hans Zimmer's score comes up on the background of an explosion. It's much more over-the-top and bombastic than COD 4 ever was, which says a lot about how campaigns will devolve into later entries, each less inovative and tactical and more action-packed/unrealistic with space combat, jetpacks and robots.

Task Force 141 is in full shape here and Shepherd makes for one hell of an antagonist. The sheer number of memorable and quoted missions is too much for the modern Call of Duty fan to comprehend, and the balls on this franchise to include an actual mission where you partake in a terrorist attack... is one for the history books. Veteran is a little more forgiving than the previous entry, but still a headache to get through, particularly the Favela missions. Otherwise, it's pretty doable if you take your time with each encounter.

On the spec-ops side of things, AIDS is the best way to describe some of them. There are those that are totally manageable on Veteran, especially solo if you don't have any friends to play with. But stuff like Wet Work, Snatch and Grab, Wardriving and the Juggernaut ones are absolutely brutal to take on by yourself. It's still a fun mode, adding extra content for those who are looking for it. But unless you have someone to accompany your quest to achieve all 69 stars... you will struggle. 

To wrap it up, Call of Duty Modern Warfare 2 is the living example of how prime COD used to look and feel. Something which unfortunately became lost to time and greed. The campaign will forever live rent-free in my head and I will always consider it the best in the series. As for Spec-Ops, it's a fun little mode you can dig into if you're looking for more, but not the most relaxing time you'll have, that's for sure. 

P.S: Don't forget to buy it ONLY if it's on sale. There isn't a single COD that is worth the full price nowadays, especially with how hacker-infested the multiplayer is.

If you're playing for the campagin then that's fine. But if you are playing for the Multiplayer then it is risky and I do not recommend it.
Some lobbies have hackers that won't let you leave lobbies/Games where you can't leave and you would have to force shut down your PC and you can't do anything to exit out of the game[Example: Alt-f4, Alt Tab, and etc will not work.] Only button that works is holding down your power button so that's the only exception.

I've played many CoD Games before but this is my first review for a CoD game. This is gonna be a very short review. This game was really good & I Enjoyed it. Great Graphics. Great story. Ending was good but a little bit okay. As with most CoD games it is short but spaced time out my time with this.  No Bugs, Crashes Nor Visual Glitches. Probably one of my top 3 CoD games ever. Definitely recommend. 8.5/10.
A masterpiece of gameplay and story. This is not a game. Its fine art

Good game. Though I wish The Green had more screentime, still ended up being a good game. What makes this game the best so far in the series is that it's the first with proper Steam achievements. I like achievements. Good game.

This is hands down one of the best COD games out of all of them. This one not only continues the story from the first modern warfare but it's so much more eventful than any of the ones I played.

I feel like the story itself was also smooth and it was always interesting from the start to finish with so much action in between, with so much chaos, it was overall just a lot of fun. Not only that the story somewhat even took a really dark turn where it escalated in a way I didn't even think about, iykyk...

All that being said, this game is extremely good and I didn't have any bugs, glitches, or anything noticeably broken from the start.

This game is a masterpiece from campaign to multiplayer to spec ops. The story is really good, I speedran this game for the longest time. I played multiplayer a lot on console and on pc at first, I would definitely avoid steam servers as you can easily be hacked within a couple hours of playtime. I'd recommend finding a third party client for the game so you can play the multiplayer. The campaign is amazing, great story. I would recommend even getting the campaign remaster on b.net for an even better looking experience. Beenox did an amazing job remastering the game but nothing beats the smoothness of the OG, even for today. I would probably not care about spending $20 on this game again if I had to.
One of the lesser cod campaigns tbh, mw3 has the better one imo.

It's good. It felt a little too "Hollywood" for me, compared to the first game, but it has many unforgettable segments. I remember being really impacted by the battles in suburban United States, was a very unique concept to that point.
This game is very cinematic, I don't surpised, how everybody love this game! Legend game!

activision is cringe for making reboots of the mw games because it makes it really hard to search for guides for this game and it pisses me off

goated game tho

Easily my favorite CoD ever, and my favorite campaign. It's extremely addicting, even on Veteran difficulty. I'm a HUGE CoD fan, and this is EASILY my favorite one, WWII being a close second. 100% worth the buy, almost ANY PC can run this game comfortably at 180fps.

Solid run and gun campaign. Some interesting twists. The came definitely shows its age. Weapons feel ok but sound sorta similar and not that realistic or satisfying. But the linearity and ability to get in the campaign quickly with fast load times helps make you want to keep playing. If you are someone that doesn't like older dated graphics then skip this.
Couldn't get it to work on my laptop, but it's an amazing game. I played it on console. It remains my favorite CoD.

I loved this story. Everything about it was top tier. The difficulty settings were not bad either, including Veteran. The games still looks great even though it came out in 2009. This is one of Call of Duty's finest entries into the franchise and I wish Activision would go back to this. My one problem is that the missions only take ~12 hours to do, but then the Special Ops stuff takes another 15 to 30 to get the Perfect Game, and I hate that. Just make the actual story longer and less side content that is a bother for people who do not have a partner to play co-op with. Overall everyone should give this game a try!

I dont have much to say but this game was one of my favorite in my childhood, really suggest to play this game at least on time in your life..

Bought this game again on steam to reminisce the good old days.
Surely one of the best games i've ever played and the best call of duty

such a good game. WaW MW2 MW3 BO and BO2 the goats the games that will live on for ever unlike the recent slop the trash they should be ashamed for what they have made in the recent years. shame the multiplayer does not work sad days but on the bright side works really good on linux

One of the best childhood game i still play. the story was so amazing but the remaster addition has made the story more appealing but their more glitches and oppunties you can still do in the og verison. the Story is so good i played it a lot and the spec ops on this is wow. the spec ops is why i rebough the game sicne it SO PEAK.

The story of the campaign is great, i can see why people praise older cods' stories. but damn, the gameplay sucks compared to newer games. I have never had a problem playing the campaigns in veteran difficulty from BO3 to BO7, but here it was just hell. the guns feel incredibly clunky and and it's really difficult to know where you're getting shot from before dying. and the damage flinching is also really extreme. If you're getting shot, good luck hitting anything.
one of if not the best cod campaigns and the multiplayer was top 3

If you want an action packed game that makes you feel like a badass even though your a 30 year old living in your moms basement, this game is perfect. the campaign is perfect and every mission is just great. i also enjoy how it gives you a warning about no Russian. i haven't done multiplayer much (mostly because of try hard 12 year olds) but what ive played its verry good. i would 100% recommended and this is the best COD game without a doubt.

I lowkey thought Call of Duty was only the Online PvP until I actually started researching a bit and found out there was campaign as well. I played nothing but the campaign so I can't say anything about the other game modes but omg this game was so well made. Sure, sometimes things were a little frustrating but generally, the story was sooo good and I fell in love with the characters. This game was an emotional rollercoster (all in a good way). I could have never imagined that a war/shooter game could be this freaking awesome!

Also a little heads-up before you buy it: Depending on which region you are from, you could potentially only get a censored version which would therefore also result in you getting the game in that region's language and you can not change it unless you mod it. So maybe consider that before you buy it if you don't like dubbed game versions.
